commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e2be8eb71986d4417e5973e307c17d7126e35773)
commands.review_summary:
The branch removes default Codex app-server mirrored-history projection and updates hook, diagnostic, and regression tests so only active non-legacy context engines supply history to Codex model-input surfaces.

PR surface: Source -75, Tests -39. Total -114 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: append mirrored session messages, start default Codex without an active context engine or usable binding, and current main can project that history into turn/start input. I did not run the harness because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer-labeled PR needs human/owner review after rebase because it removes a Codex runtime fallback and lacks real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(codex): stop injecting mirrored history into prompts
target.body:
## Summary

Stop default Codex app-server turns from injecting the mirrored OpenClaw transcript back into the Codex prompt when no active non-legacy context engine is selected.

The important split is:

- Codex native app-server threads own Codex conversation continuity.
- The mirrored OpenClaw transcript is persistence/search/display state for default Codex runs.
- Explicit non-legacy context-engine projection still owns the alternate path that assembles OpenClaw history into Codex input.

## Detailed explainer

Before this change, a default Codex run without an active context engine could still call the context-engine projection helper and rewrite the current prompt with mirrored OpenClaw history. That fallback was intended as a bridge for transient/native-disabled startup states, but it means default Codex can receive a second shadow transcript even though Codex already maintains its own thread state.

That is the source of the context bloat: the OpenClaw mirror contains message stubs and historical records that are useful for OpenClaw bookkeeping, but are not the authoritative Codex conversation. Re-injecting them into the current turn makes the prompt larger, can duplicate history, and can pollute prompt/cache behavior without adding real Codex thread continuity.

This PR removes that default fallback and makes the behavior explicit:

- Default/no-context-engine Codex runs pass no mirrored history into before_prompt_build.
- Default/no-context-engine llm_input hook payloads report no mirrored history.
- Captured model-call diagnostics use the same model-input history as the actual Codex turn, so diagnostics do not imply stale mirrored context was sent.
- Turn-start failure snapshots still preserve the OpenClaw-side mirrored messages for failure reporting/persistence; that is not model input.
- Active non-legacy context-engine projection remains supported and unchanged.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.hooks.test.ts -t "fires llm_input|emits gated model-call content diagnostics|applies before_prompt_build|does not inject mirrored history|does not inject newer mirrored history|does not project mirrored messages|stale thread-bootstrap|reads and compares thread-bootstrap"`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.hooks.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt-file /tmp/codex-mirror-projection-autoreview.md`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: default Codex app-server turns no longer inject mirrored OpenClaw transcript history into Codex prompt/model-input surfaces unless an active non-legacy context engine explicitly projects it.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Codex extension Vitest harness in this checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the focused Vitest command, diff whitespace check, oxfmt check, and local autoreview commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 8 tests across 4 files; diff check and oxfmt passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: tests assert default Codex startup/resume, before_prompt_build, llm_input, and model-call diagnostics do not contain mirrored prior context, while active context-engine projection tests remain covered.

What was not tested: full repository CI was not run locally before PR creation.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
